verses.text: Cornelius, a devout centurion, sees a vision; 'thy prayers and thine alms are come up... before God.' Heard prayer. God notices the seeking Gentile.
Cornelius, a devout centurion, sees a vision; 'thy prayers and thine alms are come up... before God.' Heard prayer. God notices the seeking Gentile.
Cornelius, a devout centurion, sees a vision; 'thy prayers and thine alms are come up... before God.' Heard prayer. God notices the seeking Gentile.
Cornelius, a devout centurion, sees a vision; 'thy prayers and thine alms are come up... before God.' Heard prayer. God notices the seeking Gentile.
He is told to send to Joppa for Peter, and obeys at once. Prompt obedience. He acts on the vision without delay.
He is told to send to Joppa for Peter, and obeys at once. Prompt obedience. He acts on the vision without delay.
He is told to send to Joppa for Peter, and obeys at once. Prompt obedience. He acts on the vision without delay.
He is told to send to Joppa for Peter, and obeys at once. Prompt obedience. He acts on the vision without delay.
Peter, praying, falls into a trance and sees a sheet of all manner of beasts. A strange vision. God prepares him for a new lesson.
Peter, praying, falls into a trance and sees a sheet of all manner of beasts. A strange vision. God prepares him for a new lesson.
Peter, praying, falls into a trance and sees a sheet of all manner of beasts. A strange vision. God prepares him for a new lesson.
Peter, praying, falls into a trance and sees a sheet of all manner of beasts. A strange vision. God prepares him for a new lesson.
'Rise... kill, and eat'; not so, Lord; 'What God hath cleansed, that call not... common,' done thrice. Old barriers fall. God declares the Gentiles clean.
'Rise... kill, and eat'; not so, Lord; 'What God hath cleansed, that call not... common,' done thrice. Old barriers fall. God declares the Gentiles clean.
'Rise... kill, and eat'; not so, Lord; 'What God hath cleansed, that call not... common,' done thrice. Old barriers fall. God declares the Gentiles clean.
'Rise... kill, and eat'; not so, Lord; 'What God hath cleansed, that call not... common,' done thrice. Old barriers fall. God declares the Gentiles clean.
While Peter doubts, the men come; the Spirit says, 'Go with them, doubting nothing.' Clear leading. God removes his hesitation.
While Peter doubts, the men come; the Spirit says, 'Go with them, doubting nothing.' Clear leading. God removes his hesitation.
While Peter doubts, the men come; the Spirit says, 'Go with them, doubting nothing.' Clear leading. God removes his hesitation.
While Peter doubts, the men come; the Spirit says, 'Go with them, doubting nothing.' Clear leading. God removes his hesitation.
Peter asks their errand; they tell of Cornelius, warned by an angel. Two visions meet. God prepares both sides for the meeting.
Peter asks their errand; they tell of Cornelius, warned by an angel. Two visions meet. God prepares both sides for the meeting.
Peter asks their errand; they tell of Cornelius, warned by an angel. Two visions meet. God prepares both sides for the meeting.
Cornelius falls at his feet; Peter raises him, 'I myself also am a man.' No man-worship. The apostle refuses undue honour.
Cornelius falls at his feet; Peter raises him, 'I myself also am a man.' No man-worship. The apostle refuses undue honour.
Cornelius falls at his feet; Peter raises him, 'I myself also am a man.' No man-worship. The apostle refuses undue honour.
Cornelius falls at his feet; Peter raises him, 'I myself also am a man.' No man-worship. The apostle refuses undue honour.
God has shewed me 'not to call any man common or unclean'; so I came. Barrier broken. Peter sets aside the old separation.
God has shewed me 'not to call any man common or unclean'; so I came. Barrier broken. Peter sets aside the old separation.
Cornelius tells his vision; 'now... are we all here present before God, to hear all things.' Ready hearers. They wait eagerly for the word.
Cornelius tells his vision; 'now... are we all here present before God, to hear all things.' Ready hearers. They wait eagerly for the word.
Cornelius tells his vision; 'now... are we all here present before God, to hear all things.' Ready hearers. They wait eagerly for the word.
Cornelius tells his vision; 'now... are we all here present before God, to hear all things.' Ready hearers. They wait eagerly for the word.
'God is no respecter of persons'; in every nation the God-fearing is accepted. Open gospel. Grace is for all peoples alike.
'God is no respecter of persons'; in every nation the God-fearing is accepted. Open gospel. Grace is for all peoples alike.
The word of peace by Jesus Christ; 'who went about doing good.' The Saviour's life. Peter preaches the kindly, mighty Christ.
The word of peace by Jesus Christ; 'who went about doing good.' The Saviour's life. Peter preaches the kindly, mighty Christ.
The word of peace by Jesus Christ; 'who went about doing good.' The Saviour's life. Peter preaches the kindly, mighty Christ.
They slew Him; God raised Him the third day, seen of chosen witnesses. The risen Lord. The apostles testify to the resurrection.
They slew Him; God raised Him the third day, seen of chosen witnesses. The risen Lord. The apostles testify to the resurrection.
They slew Him; God raised Him the third day, seen of chosen witnesses. The risen Lord. The apostles testify to the resurrection.
He bade us preach that 'whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.' Free pardon. Faith in Christ brings forgiveness to all.
He bade us preach that 'whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.' Free pardon. Faith in Christ brings forgiveness to all.
While Peter spoke, 'the Holy Ghost fell on all,' and the Gentiles spake with tongues. Spirit on the Gentiles. God seals their faith openly.
While Peter spoke, 'the Holy Ghost fell on all,' and the Gentiles spake with tongues. Spirit on the Gentiles. God seals their faith openly.
While Peter spoke, 'the Holy Ghost fell on all,' and the Gentiles spake with tongues. Spirit on the Gentiles. God seals their faith openly.
Can any forbid water? he commands them baptized 'in the name of the Lord.' Welcomed in. The Gentiles are received into the church.
Can any forbid water? he commands them baptized 'in the name of the Lord.' Welcomed in. The Gentiles are received into the church.
